# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a simple GitHub Actions workflow for our application CI. On every push, install dependencies, run unit tests, build the application, and deploy to our staging environment. Focus on speed - we want fast feedback for developers. We'll add security scanning later.
# Category: cicd_security

name: NodeCI Build Deploy CI
on: [push] # or * if you don't have a .gitignore in your repo, this rule will cause the workflow to run on every push (or pull request) and so does '*'. 
jobs:  
  build_and_test:   
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest         # specify runner type for GitHub Actions     // or if you have concurrency in mind as well, it can be specific versions too like self-hosted runners with the corresponding version. Defaults to Ubuntu latest here      
  
      - name: Check out code into git repository   
        uses: actions/checkout@v2  # specify runner type for GitHub Actions     // or use a workflow file if you have concurrency in mind as well, it can be specific versions too like self-hosted runners with the corresponding version. Defaults to Ubuntu latest here      
  
      - name: Use Node.js Version   
        uses: actions/setup-node@v2  # specify runner type for GitHub Actions     // or use a workflow file if you have concurrency in mind as well, it can be specific versions too like self-hosted runners with the corresponding version. Defaults to Ubuntu latest here      
  
      - name: Install Dependencies   
        run: npm ci  # specify runner type for GitHub Actions     // or use a workflow file if you have concurrency in mind as well, it can be specific versions too like self-hosted runners with the corresponding version. Defaults to Ubuntu latest here        
        
      - name: Run Unit Tests   
        run: npm test  # specify runner type for GitHub Actions     // or use a workflow file if you have concurrency in mind as well, it can be specific versions too like self-hosted runners with the corresponding version. Defaults to Ubuntu latest here      
        
      - name: Build Application   
        run: npm build  # specify runner type for GitHub Actions     // or use a workflow file if you have concurrency in mind as well, it can be specific versions too like self-hosted runners with the corresponding version. Defaults to Ubuntu latest here      
        
      - name: Deploy Application   
        uses: JamesIves/github-pages-deploy@4.1.5 # or specify runner type and branch for GitHub Actions     //  you can use a workflow file if concurrency is required too, it needs to be run on specific versions as well like self hosted version with the corresponding configurations above      
         url: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_PAGES_BRANCH }}   # specify runner type for GitHub Actions     // or have this set in a workflow file if concurrency is required too, it can be specified here (i.e., jekyll-theme-cayman1 and cakelatemp/githubpages#v2)
         force_push: true   # specify runner type for GitHub Actions     // or have this set in a workflow file if concurrency is required too, it can be specified here (i.e., jekyll-theme-cayman1 and cakelatemp/githubpages#v2)
         delete_branch: true  # specify runner type for GitHub Actions     // or have this set in a workflow file if concurrency is required too, it can be specified here (i.e., jekyll-theme-cayman1 and cakelatemp/githubpages#v2)
